mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 60% of triggered instances or signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and capital expenditure expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in > 50% of cases or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing output and corporate capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses or loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) when applied outside expansion regimes or during supply chain disruptions.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month inflation readings exceeding 0.4 percent trigger expectations of tighter monetary policy and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 55% of high-inflation trigger months or the signal fails to predict direction in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening and wage growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative returns in > 50% of occurrences or the interaction effect vanishes in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter increases in real gross domestic product reflect broad economic growth and rising corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth quarters coincide with negative S&P 500 forward returns in > 50% of cases or the signal information coefficient remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the industrial production index exceeding 0.2 percent signals contracting manufacturing activity and weakening corporate revenue forecasts, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances or the signal fails to achieve statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.
